en_body:
"I've tasted the heat of the stars and oh all of them tasted sweet!"
 (Hoàng-Ân)

.....I have tasted the passionate warmth of the stars and the flavor of light upon my body tasted meltingly sweet with the drunken Love of Life. I have stretched toward the high vault of sky embracing the sun that nourishes Life with blueness reminding humankind to reach into the principle of Truth-Goodness-Beauty! ...and oh how ecstatical is the strong-vigorous Life-force of Passion.

.....this world: with sky and clouds and wind, with the ivory-jewel satellite named moon and stars...and Art sublimating Grace is the passion for Creation, as though inheriting the principle of the Creator when creating this immense universe...

.....sound and movement create this mortal world intoxicated with living beings...meaning and emotion like a boat floating upon water, like a kite filled with wind stretching its body to soar high...

.....and today I suddenly understand within the diaphanous light of the sun while reading Thoreau, "the sun is a day star." taste, then, the sensation of the miraculous universe...drunken upon the joy drenched in gold of the day star nearest to the earth, benevolently nourishing all beings, like grasses and flowers, like birds and beasts, like humankind so filled with right-and-wrong yet everyone hungers to live, hungers for joy, hungers to savor fervent Life...Life is given that we may live this gracious blessing wholly...

.....stars and mountains, and a cup of wine, a cup of tea...and Poetry, with Music, with Paintings of echoes and colors: beneath the vault of heaven and within the light of the day star together with night-heaven's constellations...glittering and shimmering the clear source of healing water...may this manifold Life be sublimated: "suffering is sunlight...helping the teardrop become vapor returning..." (Phạm Thiên Thư)
note: The poem joins Thoreau's image of the sun as a day star with Phạm Thiên Thư's Buddhist-inflected verse on suffering and transformation.
